location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in Nashville office

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in RevOps, BizOps, management consulting, banking, GTM strategy, or GTM Engineering
Comp range $200K-$240K base, consistent with Senior Manager at a high-growth startup
Title is Senior Manager; no explicit people-management responsibility stated in JD

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience in RevOps, BizOps, management consulting, banking, GTM strategy, or GTM Engineering, ideally supporting high-growth B2B SaaS organizations.
must_haves.qualifier: ideally supporting high-growth B2B SaaS organizations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Advanced analytical skills: expertise working with large and sometimes unstructured data sets, building complex models, and translating analysis into clear, actionable recommendations.
Genuine interest in using and building with AI in practical, high-leverage ways to improve workflows, analysis, and decision-making
Excellent communication skills: ability to navigate and communicate complex ideas effectively verbally, in writing, and through polished presentations for senior audiences
Strong operator mindset: you can move from problem definition to recommendation to implementation
Ability to thrive in an action‑oriented, fast‑paced, work environment; you're comfortable with ambiguity and motivated by building structure where it doesn't yet exist.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: ideally supporting high-growth B2B SaaS organizations. We welcome candidates with transferable analytical and operational skill sets, even if they have not held a formal RevOps title before

scope.surface: Global Revenue Operations — sales planning, forecasting, reporting, process design across go-to-market organization
core_responsibilities: Shape how Revenue Operations uses AI to improve workflows, reporting, decision-making, and team leverage
Own global reporting and analyses across all sales funnel metrics, segment performance, and pipeline modeling
Lead key annual planning workstreams with Field Operations segment partners, including book building, territory design, and geo analyses
Partner closely with Sales, Marketing, Post-Sales, Finance, and Systems leaders, owning cross-functional operational cadences
Lead the design and build of reporting and dashboarding with an AI-first approach
Work with Systems to drive CRM hygiene and scalability for global reporting
Act as a thought partner to cross-functional leadership, bringing structured analyses, clear recommendations, and a POV

compensation.equity_form: Certain roles may be eligible for variable compensation, equity, and benefits

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($200K-$240K base)
JD explicitly welcomes candidates without a formal RevOps title if they have transferable analytical/operational skills
Clear scope of responsibilities and cross-functional partners listed
AI-first culture with structured AI fluency assessment as part of interview process — signals genuine AI adoption, not just marketing
Glean has strong industry recognition (Fast Company Top 10, Forbes AI 50, CNBC Disruptor 50)
red_flags: 4 days per week in Nashville office is a near-onsite hybrid commitment — eliminates most remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Manages people is not stated but 'Senior Manager' title could imply future team-building expectations that aren't surfaced in requirements
No mention of specific tooling (e.g., Salesforce, Tableau, dbt) despite CRM hygiene and dashboarding being core to the role

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false from JD text which does not reference direct reports)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly quoted in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and 'high-growth B2B SaaS' language)

ambiguities: The role is titled 'Senior Manager' but no people management responsibilities are mentioned in the JD body. It is unclear whether this is a senior IC or a people manager role.
Variable compensation is mentioned as possible ('Certain roles may be eligible') but it is not confirmed for this role specifically.
B2B SaaS industry experience is described as 'ideally' preferred, making it borderline must-have vs. nice-to-have; classified as nice-to-have since the JD explicitly welcomes candidates without it.
